finding: May be associated with: asbestos exposure

differentials.name: weight loss, nausea, malaise, anorexia, low-grade fever
differentials.raw: weight loss, nausea, malaise, anorexia, low-grade fever

differentials.name: dull pain in costovertebral angle, back, abdomen
differentials.raw: dull pain in costovertebral angle, back, abdomen (90%)

differentials.name: obstructive uropathy → oliguria → anuria → renal insufficiency
differentials.raw: obstructive uropathy → oliguria → anuria → renal insufficiency (50-60%)

differentials.name: renal vessel involvement (2-35%) → hypertension → renal insufficiency
differentials.raw: renal vessel involvement (2-35%) → hypertension → renal insufficiency

differentials.name: lymphatic + venous obstruction → lower extremity edema, deep vein thrombosis (6%), hydrocele (10%), scrotal swelling, varicocele
differentials.raw: lymphatic + venous obstruction → lower extremity edema, deep vein thrombosis (6%), hydrocele (10%), scrotal swelling, varicocele

differentials.name: compression of arteries → bowel ischemia, claudication
differentials.raw: compression of arteries → bowel ischemia, claudication (occasionally)

differentials.name: high erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR), anemia
differentials.raw: high erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR), anemia

differentials.name: high levels of C-reactive protein
differentials.raw: high levels of C-reactive protein (80-100%)

differentials.name: tests for antinuclear antibodies may be positive Location:   plaque typically begins around aortic bifurcation (L4-5) extending cephalad to renal hilum / surrounding kidney, duodenum, pancreas and spleen; rarely extends below pelvic rim, but may extend caudad to bladder + rectosigmoid Retroperitoneal fibrosis most commonly surrounds the infrarenal abdominal aorta and proximal common iliac arteries.
differentials.raw: tests for antinuclear antibodies may be positive Location:   plaque typically begins around aortic bifurcation (L4-5) extending cephalad to renal hilum / surrounding kidney, duodenum, pancreas and spleen; rarely extends below pelvic rim, but may extend caudad to bladder + rectosigmoid Retroperitoneal fibrosis most commonly surrounds the infrarenal abdominal aorta and proximal common iliac arteries.

differentials.name: Ureterectasis above L4-5 ← interference with peristalsis
differentials.raw: Ureterectasis above L4-5 ← interference with peristalsis

differentials.name: Medial deviation of ureters in middle third, typically bilateral = maiden waist deformity
differentials.raw: Medial deviation of ureters in middle third, typically bilateral = maiden waist deformity

differentials.name: Gradual tapering of ureters at lower lumbar spine / upper sacral region ← extrinsic compression DDx:  primary ureteral tumor, periureteral lymph nodes, inflammatory ureteral stricture
differentials.raw: Gradual tapering of ureters at lower lumbar spine / upper sacral region ← extrinsic compression DDx:  primary ureteral tumor, periureteral lymph nodes, inflammatory ureteral stricture

differentials.name: Perianeurysmal fibrosis
differentials.raw: Perianeurysmal fibrosis (dilated encased aorta)

differentials.name: Malignant retroperitoneal fibrosis / sarcoma
differentials.raw: Malignant retroperitoneal fibrosis / sarcoma (mass with irregular lobular / nodular margins, more cephalad location, anterior displacement of aorta, edema, Doppler flow, contrast enhancement)

differentials.name: Retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy, lymphoma
differentials.raw: Retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy, lymphoma (anterior elevation of aorta / IVC off vertebral body)

differentials.name: Primary amyloidosis
differentials.raw: Primary amyloidosis

differentials.name: Subacute retroperitoneal hematoma
differentials.raw: Subacute retroperitoneal hematoma

differentials.name: Withdrawal of possible causative agent
differentials.raw: Withdrawal of possible causative agent

differentials.name: Interventional relief of urinary obstruction
differentials.raw: Interventional relief of urinary obstruction

differentials.name: Corticosteroids / immunotherapeutic drugs
differentials.raw: Corticosteroids / immunotherapeutic drugs

differentials.name: Disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs, tamoxifen
differentials.raw: Disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs, tamoxifen

differentials.name: Ureterolysis for refractory cases
differentials.raw: Ureterolysis for refractory cases

context: √  tethering of aorta / IVC to vertebrae without anterior displacement √  usually mild uni- / bilateral pyelocaliectasis → hydronephrosis → renal failure (56-100%) ◊  Diagnostic clue: incongruity between severe degree of renal failure + mild degree of hydronephrosis! IVP / retrograde pyelography: √  Classic TRIAD: US (poor sensitivity): √  hypo- / isoechoic well-demarcated irregularly contoured homogeneous mass with smooth margins anterior to lower lumbar spine / sacral promontory √  varying degrees of uni- / bilateral hydronephrosis √  NO flow on Doppler imaging CT: √  well-delimited irregula
